messages.text: OFW ping came in while I was parking at the office. It reads procedural on the surface, but my body is treating it like a custody flare. I’m not responding before clients.
David sent another very calm, very procedural custody note, which somehow makes me feel more braced. I’m trying not to read tone into every comma and failing.
Barely slept. I know this sounds paranoid, but I keep feeling like he still has eyes on everything. I’m so tired of living like a case file.
He just referenced “past stability concerns” in OFW, minutes before my first client. I’m not going to engage with the bait, but I need guidance on whether this needs a formal response.
Uploading the screenshot summary now. Can you tell me whether silence is safer here, or whether we should answer narrowly so it doesn’t look like avoidance?
I drafted a whole OFW reply and then had the rare good sense not to send it. It’s living in Notes until my prefrontal cortex returns.
On our call, I’d like help documenting this without sounding defensive. I want the record to show I’m child-focused, not reactive to his insinuations.
Sent the OFW response. Kept it to school logistics and the schedule, no commentary. It felt cold, but probably appropriately cold.
Call me when the kids are asleep. You don’t have to be elegant about any of this with me. Just dump it out and we’ll sort the pieces.
Can we walk after work instead of texting about custody? I don’t want more of my panic preserved in writing for the litigation gods.
School nurse says Eli has a sore throat and may need pickup later. I’m already anticipating David turning this into a competence referendum, which is absurd and also exactly where my brain goes.
Heads up, I may need to move afternoon admin if the school calls about Eli. Nothing dramatic, just parenting logistics wearing a tiny legal hat.
I sent David a brief OFW update: fever, pediatrician guidance, proposed handoff adjustment. No extra explanation. Please tell me if I accidentally sounded like a deposition exhibit.
Thermometer photo attached. I am too tired to parse whether David’s tone is normal-co-parent or setting-a-trap co-parent. Please calibrate me.
Responded in OFW with medication timing and the pediatrician’s instructions only. I left out my feelings, which are plentiful and currently unhelpful.
At this point I need a paralegal, a bookkeeper, and a nervous system. Ideally all bundled into one mensch with childcare experience.
Just FaceTimed the kids. Eli sounds better, thank God. I kept it light, but I wanted to cry after because I miss the ordinary version of us.
Sent you a voice memo because if I type it I’ll turn it into a deposition. Trying very hard to answer OFW like a regulated adult and not write six paragraphs proving I’m a good mother.

Eli fever timeline: Sunday 6:40pm 100.8, Tylenol, slept through; Monday 7:15am 99.1, kept home per school guidance; pickup window issue was David arriving 18 min after stated time. I can send screenshots if useful.

I am concerned that framing a missed handoff window as instability ignores the actual medical context and Eli’s needs. I want us to keep the children’s routine predictable without turning every sick day into a custody exhibit.

Proposed OFW reply for your eyes before I send: 'Eli was home due to fever consistent with school policy. I will provide documentation as available. I intend to follow the regular schedule unless we both agree otherwise in writing.' Too much?
Eli was home due to fever consistent with school policy. I will provide documentation as available. I intend to follow the regular schedule unless we both agree otherwise in writing.

Embarrassed by how much one OFW message can hijack my whole nervous system. I can literally feel my jaw lock before I even finish reading it. Therapist brain knows what’s happening; body is not interested in the memo.
OFW stayed quiet today. I’m trying to let that count as a win instead of scanning for the next thing. Extremely advanced wellness content over here.
Hi, Eli is back to baseline this morning and should be able to participate normally. Could you let me know if there was a worksheet or packet he should complete from yesterday? Thanks so much.
I’m going to bail on coffee, I’m sorry. I have absolutely no social bandwidth and I’d rather be honest than sit there performing okay-ness at you.
Sending screenshots. Do you remember who was paying for iCloud storage before everything split? I hate that I can’t tell what’s mine, what’s the family plan, and what David can still see.
Do not do legal docs after wine. Do not reread old drafts. Do not search Adam’s name, Chatham, or anything from 2022 just because you feel exposed. Sleep first, decide tomorrow.
This account cleanup is making me feel so foolish. Like every shared folder is a little receipt for how much I trusted him to be decent. I know that’s not the whole story, but today it feels like it.
I’m spiraling about the house. The refinance, the kids’ rooms, whether keeping them in the Victorian is actually stability or just me clinging to the last intact-looking thing.
For Monday: Maya’s blue backpack and Eli’s medication will be by the front door. Pickup remains 7:45 unless you propose a different time through OFW by noon today.
Hi, I’m checking in on the refinance file. Could you confirm which documents are still outstanding on my end? I want to make sure I’m not holding up review.
Made the calls I was avoiding. Credit union, insurance, dentist. I’m still cooked, but less feral. Please remind me later that avoidance was louder than the actual tasks.
Slept like absolute garbage, but I’m keeping today boring and procedural. No extra words for David, no spiraling in writing. Please remind me I said that if I start drafting a novella.
Confirmed. I will have Maya and Eli ready for pickup at 5:30. Eli’s inhaler and the school forms will be in his backpack.
I still do not have complete medical documentation for Eli. Please send everything today so there is no confusion about what you are withholding or forgetting.
Voice note: I can’t tell if this is actually about medical paperwork or if he’s laying down little custody breadcrumbs. I need another nervous system to read the room before I answer.
Attached is Eli’s illness summary from this week, including symptoms, medication times, and the nurse call. I will provide any additional school documentation when available.
Hi Dana, I attached the OFW medical packet I plan to send. Could you let me know if the phrasing needs anything else, or if shorter is better here? Trying to keep this strictly factual.
Small victory: I did not overexplain. Four sentences, one attachment, zero therapy-language garnish. I am pretending this counts as personal growth.
Kid-free weekend starts tomorrow and somehow the house paperwork pile feels louder than the children. I’m embarrassed by how much I’ve avoided, even though I know shame is not a filing system.
Hi Mark, could you confirm the exact deadline for the remaining refinance documents and whether PDFs exported from Google Drive are acceptable? I want to avoid sending anything in the wrong format.
Confirming transition today at 5:30 at your house. Both backpacks will include school folders, Eli’s medication, and Maya’s library book.
I have become a part-time archivist of my own divorce. Very niche career pivot, terrible benefits, lots of scanning receipts while muttering clinically appropriate profanity.
FaceTime was good. I’m okay, really. Just trying to get refinance things organized. For July, could you maybe come the second week instead of the first? I don’t want to make it a whole production.

Sending screenshots of Apple ID settings. Which step is least likely to blow something up or alert David? I want out of the family-plan weirdness, but I’m afraid of making the invisible stuff visible.
Draft not sent: Please confirm whether you agree to reimburse 50% of the summer camp balance by June 28, per the prior agreement. I can upload the receipt here if needed.

Refi packet is officially submitted and my body is acting like I mailed my nervous system to underwriting. I’m shaky but functional. Please remind me this is just paperwork, not a verdict on whether I get to keep the house.
Eli’s medication can be picked up at the pharmacy after 3. His school folder is in the front pocket of his backpack. Please confirm once you have both.
Already activated and I have six clients stacked like a tiny emotional Jenga tower. Trying to stay therapist-shaped and not let the custody/refi dread leak into the room.
I’m still waiting for reimbursement on summer camp. It would be helpful if you could respond promptly instead of creating another ambiguity around shared expenses.

David sent another camp reimbursement note this morning. Do you want my reply to simply confirm receipt/payment timing, or should I reference the temporary order language? I don’t want to over-explain and create exhibit material.
Sending screenshot. Does this credit union wording sound normal-ominous or actually ominous? I can’t tell if I’m reading English or just projecting foreclosure vibes onto a PDF.
Per the current order, I will reimburse my share of agreed summer camp expenses within the required timeframe after receiving documentation. Please upload the receipt here so the record is complete.
Lunch break check-in: trying not to catastrophize the appraisal. My brain keeps turning one pending review into me packing the kids’ rooms in banker boxes. Very chill, very embodied.

Going to bed, sort of. Nothing concrete happened tonight, but I still have that watched feeling, like every tab and account has a tiny David-shaped shadow on it. I hate that I sound paranoid when I’m probably only half wrong.
Your appraisal review is currently pending. No additional documents are required from you at this time. We will contact you if further information is needed.
Practical question before I touch anything: if I separate Apple accounts, can it mess with the kids’ iPads, Find My, or app purchases? I want less leakage, not a weekend tech fire.

Tomorrow checklist: change Apple ID password, review Family Sharing, check Find My devices, remove old backups if possible, screenshot before/after, and not spiral into every 2024 iMessage being evidence.

Is it reasonable to postpone legal doc review until Sunday? I can technically do it tonight, but I’m at the point where every sentence feels like a custody trap and I don’t trust my nervous system after 8.

Update: changed Apple password, checked devices, removed David from what I could see, and turned off a couple of shared things. Still unclear on old backups and the kids’ iPads, but I’m less flooded than yesterday.
Can we keep tonight to housing fear and no new decisions? I need to say out loud that I’m scared of losing the house without accidentally creating a seventeen-step plan at 8pm.

I’m weirdly exposed waiting on the bank, like one more person is about to see the whole messy undercarriage. Trying not to spiral, but I hate that David may still have windows into things I never meant to leave open.

Drafted a response re: camp reimbursement language but I’m holding it. The prior thread feels like a trap, and I don’t want to create another exhibit by trying to sound reasonable at the wrong moment.

OFW just pinged about summer pickup wording. I’m leaving it unread until after sessions because I can feel my body doing the Friday-night alarm thing even though it’s morning. Please don’t let me answer from panic.

I think I found another stupid shared-folder landmine. Possibly old consult/prep stuff tied to the family domain. Can you do a quick gut check later, quietly? I’m trying to separate actual risk from David-shaped paranoia.

Before I change permissions on old Drive folders, could that create any issue with discovery obligations? I’m not deleting anything; I just want to stop access that probably should have ended years ago.

calendar.summary: Home care morning; farmers market tentative
Call credit union, Apple Support, and Jennifer Yao
Monday lighter client block - phone on Do Not Disturb in office drawer
Stacked Tuesday sessions - no digital activity until break
Consultation group from home office - camera off first ten minutes
Afternoon clinical block - phone on Do Not Disturb
Lighter Friday client block - digitally quiet during sessions
Review Apple ID family sharing settings
OFW check, credit union follow-up, Apple support call
Tentative call with Jennifer Yao about refinance timeline and account cleanup questions
